title: The Spanish Alphabet
revisionOf: curriculum-architect 2026-05-16 (27 triggers reduced to 17 — runtime-shape fixes + orphan removal)
notes: Revised Wave 2 manifest. Every trigger maps to an existing TouchableOpacity in the rendered UI. Two fields are build-script-only and must NOT be copied into modules.ts when the app-engineer wires Module 1: `ttsInputText` (the actual text fed to OpenAI — may differ from `text` for pronunciation reasons) and `speed`. The runtime `TtsTrigger` type only has: id, screen, text, language, slowVersion, normalVersion, outputFile, mvpRequired. The `text` field MUST equal the vocab row `spanish` value (or the phraseChunk string, or the reading-token text) exactly — that is what PreListenScreen's findVocabTrigger / findPhraseTrigger looks up. Removed from curriculum-architect's draft: 10 standalone vowel triggers (a/e/i/o/u × 2), 2 enye-demo triggers, 2 redundant chain triggers consolidated — none of those map to a wired tap target. Vowel ladder is a single chain trigger per row since the vocab row text IS 'a, e, i, o, u'. Each non-vowel digraph/letter uses a single trigger per (slow|normal) where the ttsInputText embeds the demo word so the audio is intelligible. UI screen-text mismatch flagged: enrichment.vocabPack.phraseChunk is 'Se escribe ___.' but I'm shipping audio for 'Bésame mucho.' — content-builder should align enrichment to match, or this trigger needs to be 'Se escribe ___.' literally.

ttsTriggers.voiceStyle: deliberate, pedagogical — each vowel held briefly with a small pause
ttsTriggers.pronunciationNotes: The 5 pure Spanish vowels chanted in sequence: ah-eh-ee-oh-oo. Ellipses force ~0.4s pause between each. Output must NOT contain English diphthongs (no /eɪ/ for e, no /aɪ/ for i, no /juː/ for u). If TTS produces any glide, regenerate using es-419 hint in input punctuation.
ttsTriggers.qaFlag: highest-risk — listen for diphthongization, especially e→/eɪ/ and i→/aɪ/

ttsTriggers.voiceStyle: natural cadence — like saying the vowels casually, not chanting
ttsTriggers.pronunciationNotes: Same chain at natural speed. Shorter pauses (~0.15s).

ttsTriggers.voiceStyle: clear, deliberate — letter name first, then demo word
ttsTriggers.pronunciationNotes: Spanish letter name 'eñe' (EH-nyeh), then the demo word 'mañana' (ma-NYA-na). Both contain the palatal 'ny'. We feed both into the API in one call so the learner hears the name and an immediate real-word anchor. Total audio ~2.5s.
ttsTriggers.qaFlag: confirm clean palatal 'ny' in both name and demo

ttsTriggers.pronunciationNotes: Digraph name 'doble ele' followed by canonical demo 'llamar' (ya-MAR, es-419 yeísmo — never the Argentine 'sh').
ttsTriggers.qaFlag: must NOT produce regional 'sh' allophone — confirm clean 'y' palatal

ttsTriggers.voiceStyle: clear, pedagogical — gentle emphasis on the silent H
ttsTriggers.pronunciationNotes: Letter name 'hache' then the rule 'siempre muda' (always silent), then the demo 'hola'. The output for 'hola' MUST begin with the vowel /o/ — no /h/ aspiration. Most-likely-to-fail trigger in the manifest.
ttsTriggers.qaFlag: critical — confirm zero aspiration on 'hola' and 'hache'

ttsTriggers.pronunciationNotes: Digraph name 'doble erre' (which itself contains a trilled rr) then demo 'perro'. Both must clearly trill. tts-1-hd under-trills — regenerate up to 3 times if first take is weak. NOTE on Module 4 contrast-perro-slow: I am generating a FRESH copy for Module 1 per lead direction — Metro require() paths are module-specific and cross-module sharing breaks the bundler.
ttsTriggers.qaFlag: trill must be audible — regenerate if first take sounds like a single tap

ttsTriggers.voiceStyle: warm, natural — trill present but relaxed
warm, intimate, bolero-adjacent — slightly slower than conversational, tender but not theatrical
ttsTriggers.pronunciationNotes: MODULE CENTERPIECE — the phrase chunk IS the song title. Stress on first syllable: BÉ-sa-me. Soft B (Spanish B/V are nearly indistinguishable bilabials). 'mucho' = MOO-cho, pure U. The findPhraseTrigger lookup will fall through to the id.startsWith('phrase-') branch, since enrichment.vocabPack.phraseChunk currently reads 'Se escribe ___.' — see UI mismatch flag in notes.
ttsTriggers.uiMismatchFlag: enrichment.vocabPack.phraseChunk = 'Se escribe ___.' but audio = 'Bésame mucho.'. Content-builder should update enrichment to 'Bésame mucho.' so displayed text matches audio. Alternative: change this trigger's text to 'Se escribe ___.' and ttsInputText to 'Se escribe be-é-sa-me.' — but loses song-title anchor.

ttsTriggers.voiceStyle: narration tone — title-of-a-song delivery, slightly reverent
ttsTriggers.pronunciationNotes: Reading passage token (modules.ts:225). Title-case 'Bésame Mucho' as a proper title. Distinct from the lowercase imperative phrase trigger above.

ttsTriggers.voiceStyle: warm, conversational — as a real greeting
ttsTriggers.pronunciationNotes: Reading passage token (modules.ts:227). Sentence-initial Capital H, still silent. Output begins with vowel /o/.

ttsTriggers.voiceStyle: warm, lyric-quoting — as if quoting a line from the song
ttsTriggers.pronunciationNotes: Reading passage token (modules.ts:229). Lowercase verb form. Phonetic 'BEH-sa-meh'. Pure 'eh' on é.

ttsTriggers.voiceStyle: warm, pedagogical — used as a sound example in the narrative
ttsTriggers.pronunciationNotes: Reading passage token (modules.ts:231). Phonetic 'yo-RAR'. LL=Y rule demo. Single R between vowels is a tap, not trill.
Reading passage token (modules.ts:233). Phonetic 'ah-BLAR'. Silent-H rule demo. Output MUST begin with vowel /a/.

costEstimate.breakdown: 2 vowel-chain + 2 enye + 2 ll + 2 h + 2 rr + 1 phrase + 5 reading = 16 triggers, 16 MP3 files.
